content.heading: The Brief
content.body: A restaurant and bar for one of Kampala's most prominent hospitality operators — a venue that needed to succeed at lunch, dinner, and late-night, across two distinct but connected spaces: a 60-cover restaurant and a separate bar with its own character and entrance.

content.caption: The restaurant dining room — warm, layered, and deliberately unhurried.

items.heading: Restaurant
items.body: The dining room uses banquette seating along three walls, creating intimate clusters even when fully occupied. Pendant lighting at varying heights creates a ceiling plane that draws the eye without dominating. The kitchen is partially open — visible from a theatre rail along one side.

items.caption: The bar — backlit shelving, a marble counter, and a deliberately darker atmosphere.

content.heading: The Bar
content.body: The bar occupies a separate room with its own entrance from the street, allowing it to run independently of the restaurant. Dark fluted panelling, a long marble counter, and backlit shelving create a space that is moodier and more nocturnal in character — a deliberate contrast to the warmth of the dining room next door.
